channel.title: Steve Hill's Blog
channel.description: Thoughts on software engineering, technology, and more.

item.title: Building a ROM Collection Manager: Where Domain Knowledge Meets Multi-Threaded Rust
item.description: The technical challenges were solvable. The harder part was understanding how collectors actually work - the DAT ecosystem, verification workflows, and organisational patterns that had to shape every architectural decision.

item.title: Shipping a Rails 8 Book Tracker in a Day (With Claude Code Doing the Heavy Lifting)
item.description: AI-assisted development is fast. Surprisingly fast. But 'fast' hides decisions that AI can't make for you - scope, taste, and the stuff that only matters when you're the one who has to use it.

item.title: Teaching Assembly Programming (With AI Writing the First Drafts)
item.description: I'm building an assembly programming education platform. AI generates the first drafts. Every lesson will be rewritten. The honesty is the point.

item.title: Building Shiki Highlight API: How Development Workflow Saved My Syntax Highlighting
item.description: From 389 console errors to zero: building a high-performance syntax highlighter with CSS Custom Highlights API, Shiki, and Claude Code—and learning that workflow matters more than I thought.

item.title: Why rescue projects fail even when you do everything right
item.description: I used to leave snarky comments in code criticizing my coworkers. Years later I came back and realized I'd been wrong. That's when I learned how to actually do rescue work.

item.title: Building Rachel Part 2: Themes, Polish, and Production Deployment
item.description: From functional card game to production-ready web application: implementing a dynamic theme system, fixing critical bugs, and deploying to the world with bank-grade security.

item.title: Building Rachel: A Phoenix LiveView Card Game - Part 1
item.description: How I turned a 30-year-old family card game into a modern web application using Phoenix LiveView, complete with AI opponents and real-time multiplayer.

item.title: Sustainable Software
item.description: Sustainable software development isn’t just about clean code or green energy. It’s about people, pace, and pragmatic decisions that last.

item.title: Microservices, and why they're not always the answer
item.description: Microservices were supposed to save us. For most teams, they just moved the pain around — and made everything more complicated in the process.

item.title: Lead Developer London 2019
item.description: Lead Dev London 2019 brought together 1400+ engineers and leaders for two days of inspiration, practical advice, and community — still my favourite conference.

item.title: Containers
item.description: Containers like Docker transformed how we build and run software — offering reproducibility, isolation, and a path to modern deployments. Here's why I changed my mind.

item.title: Moving on from Guru Systems, joining Divido
item.description: After a great run at Guru Systems, I’m heading to Divido — a fintech company where I’ll be building out their consumer lending platform with PHP, Golang, and containers.

item.title: Deus Ex
item.description: Thoughts on Deus Ex: Mankind Divided, moral choices in cyberpunk, and why sneaking around is more fun than shooting.

item.title: Spectrum Next Update
item.description: Eighteen months after my last update, I'm still waiting for my Spectrum Next to arrive. Once it does, I'll be diving back into ZX Spectrum development.

item.title: Joining Guru Systems
item.description: After two years of contracting with Guru Systems via Siftware, I’m joining their team to keep building their platform and expand into new areas.

item.title: Getting started with ZX Spectrum Development
item.description: Inspired by Revival 2016 and the Spectrum Next Kickstarter, I set out to build a ZX Spectrum dev environment on macOS using Pasmo, Atom, and ZXSP.

item.title: Not a Ninja
item.description: On choosing proven technologies, writing maintainable code, and avoiding hype like microservices for the sake of it.

item.title: Revival Solstice 2016 and the ZX Spectrum Next
item.description: Reflections from Revival Solstice 2016, featuring talks by legendary developers and a look at the ZX Spectrum Next prototype.

item.title: Joining the team at Siftware
item.description: After nearly two years at Impact Applications, I’m making a move to join Siftware, where I’ll be helping modernise legacy PHP systems.

item.title: Elite: Dangerous
item.description: Why I'm so excited about Elite: Dangerous

item.title: Why I like Ruby on Rails
item.description: Reflecting on how I discovered Ruby on Rails and why it continues to be my go-to for personal projects.
